steps.title: Write the total cost expression
steps.content: Cans cost 1.87 each and bags of treats cost 2.37 each. If we buy $c$ cans and $t$ bags, the total cost is: $$1.87c + 2.37t$$

steps.title: Determine the inequality constraint
steps.content: The shopper has 20 to spend. The phrase can buy means items purchased must fall within the budget. The total cost cannot exceed 20, so we need: $$1.87c + 2.37t \leq 20$$

steps.title: Match to the answer choice
steps.content: The inequality we derived has 1.87 as the coefficient of $c$ and 2.37 as the coefficient of $t$, with a $\leq$ constraint. That matches choice A — A is correct.
